summary: Updated the README data progress badge URL to use decomp.dev's `complete_data` measure instead of `data`, fixing the displayed Data progress badge. This was a small documentation/status-badge fix with no code or decomp matching impact.

what_changed.change: Changed the `[Data Progress]` badge URL query parameter from `measure=data` to `measure=complete_data`.

smart_moves: Kept the fix minimal: one README line changed, preserving the existing badge format, label, category, and neighboring code/linked progress badge definitions.
Aligned the data progress badge measure name with the existing `complete_code` style used for linked code progress, suggesting the badge endpoint expects completion-oriented measure keys.
decomp_lessons: Progress badge URLs are part of project-facing decomp infrastructure and can become stale independently of source matching work.
When updating decomp.dev badges, verify the exact `measure` query key; similar-looking measures such as `data` and `complete_data` may not be interchangeable.
README badge maintenance should be treated as a lightweight but visible correctness fix because it affects public project status reporting.
naming_conventions: decomp.dev progress badge measure for data completion is `complete_data`.
The README badge label remains human-facing as `Data`, while the machine-readable URL measure uses `complete_data`.
Neighboring badge measures include `code` for Code Progress and `complete_code` for Linked Progress.
follow_up_queries: Find other README or documentation badge URLs using outdated decomp.dev measure parameters.
Check decomp.dev supported measure names for code, data, complete_code, and complete_data.
Audit project progress badges after infrastructure changes to avoid stale public status indicators.
